content:
The Central Limit Theorem is important in statistics because

Select one:

a.
for a large n, it says the population is approximately normal.


b.
for any sized sample, it says the sampling distribution of the sample mean is approximately normal.


c.
for any population, it says the sampling distribution of the sample mean is approximately normal, regardless of the sample size.


d.
for a large n, it says the sampling distribution of the sample mean is approximately normal, regardless of the shape of the population.
d. for a large n, it says the sampling distribution of the sample mean is approximately normal, regardless of the shape of the population.

moves.text_span: d. for a large n, it says the sampling distribution of the sample mean is approx...

content:
Which of the following is true regarding the sampling distribution of the mean for a large sample size?

Select one:

a.
It has a normal distribution with the same mean as the population but with a smaller standard deviation


b.
It has a normal distribution with the same mean and standard deviation as the population


c.
It has the same shape and mean as the population, but has a smaller standard deviation


d.
It has the same shape, mean, and standard deviation as the population
a. It has a normal distribution with the same mean as the population but with a smaller standard deviation.

moves.text_span: a. It has a normal distribution with the same mean as the population but with a...

content:
If the expected value of a sample statistic is equal to the parameter it is estimating, then the sample statistic

Select one:

a.
random.


b.
minimum variance.


c.
unbiased.


d.
biased.

moves.text_span:
e:

a.
random.


b.
minimum variance.


c.
unbiased.


d.
biased.

content: c. unbiased.

Sampling distributions describe the distribution of

Select one:

a.
population parameters.


b.
sample statistics.


c.
both population parameters and sample statistics.


d.
neither population parameters nor sample statistics.
b. sample statistics.

A sampling distribution is a distribution for a statistic

Select one:
True
False
True.

The standard error of the mean is also known as the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the sample mean

Select one:
True
False
True.

Why is the Central Limit Theorem so important to the study of sampling distributions?


Select one:

a.
It allows us to disregard the size of the sample selected when the population is not normal.


b.
It allows us to disregard the size of the population we are sampling from.



c.
It allows us to disregard the shape of the population when n is large


d.
It allows us to disregard the shape of the sampling distribution when the size of the population is large
c. It allows us to disregard the shape of the population when n is large.

For sample size 16, the sampling distribution of the mean will be approximately normally distributed

Select one:

a.
regardless of the shape of the population


b.
if the sample standard deviation is known


c.
if the shape of the population is symmetrical


d.
if the sample is not normally distributed
a. regardless of the shape of the population.

The Central Limit Theorem ensures that the sampling distribution of the sample mean approaches normal as the sample size increases

Select one:
True
False
True.

If the population distribution is unknown, in most cases the sampling distribution of the mean can be approximated by the normal distribution if the samples contain at least 30 observations

Select one:
True
False

moves.text_span:
the samples contain at least 30 observations

Select one:
True
False

content: True.

Which of the following statements about the sampling distribution of the sample mean is incorrect?

Select one:

a.
The sampling distribution of the sample mean is generated by repeatedly taking samples of size n and computing the sample means


b.
The mean of the sampling distribution of the sample mean is equal to μ


c.
The sampling distribution of the sample mean is approximately normal whenever the sample size is sufficiently large (n ≥ 30).


d.
The standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the sample mean is equal to σ
d. The standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the sample mean is equal to σ. (It should be standard deviation of the sample mean, which is known as the standard error of the mean.)

moves.text_span: d. The standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the sample mean is equ...

content:
According to the empirical rule, if the data form a "bell-shaped" normal distribution, ________ percent of the observations will be contained within 2 standard deviations around the arithmetic mean.

Select one:

a.
approximately 95%


b.
approximately 93%


c.
approximately 68%


d.
approximately 89%
a. approximately 95%

The sample mean is an unbiased estimate of the population mean

Select one:
True
False
True.

Which of the following is true about the sampling distribution of the sample mean?

Select one:

a.
All of the above are true.


b.
The shape of the sampling distribution is always approximately normal.


c.
The standard deviation of the sampling distribution is always σ


d.
The mean of the sampling distribution is always μ.

moves.text_span:
ing distribution is always approximately normal.


c.
The standard

content: d. The mean of the sampling distribution is always μ.

f the amount of gasoline purchased per car at a large service station has a population mean of $15 and a population standard deviation of $4, then 99.73% of all cars will purchase between $3 and $27

Select one:
True
False

False. (The range of $3 and $27 correspond to 3 times the population standard deviation. If the data follows a normal distribution, then approximately 99.73% of the observations would fall within 3 standard deviations around the mean. However, the statement only provides the mean and standard deviation of the population, and it doesn't specify whether the data follows a normal distribution.)
